summary_oneline: Wire a decoupled WS voice round-trip for AgentTalk: STT (existing VoiceTranscriber) -> text Agent -> synthesize AIMessage.response via VoiceSynthesizer (+ new Supertonic backend) -> return audio_base64 + structured content
synthesis_summary: AI-Parrot already ships every primitive this request needs, just not assembled for AgentTalk. A shared, symmetric voice stack lives in parrot.voice: STT (VoiceTranscriber/FasterWhisper, FEAT-039) and a decoupled TTS layer (VoiceSynthesizer + AbstractTTSBackend, FEAT-213). The canonical AIMessage already separates the speakable text (response: str) from non-speakable payloads (output/data/media), exactly validating the requester's concern about not streaming the raw AIMessage. A full aiohttp WebSocket shell with auth + sessions + base64 audio framing already exists (VoiceChatHandler), and LiveVoiceResponse.to_websocket_message defines the audio_base64 reply convention. The work is therefore enrichment, not greenfield: (1) add a Supertonic AbstractTTSBackend, (2) wire STT -> a text Agent -> synthesize(AIMessage.response) -> return {audio_base64 + content}, reusing VoiceChatHandler's transport (either as a new 'pipeline' mode or a sibling handler). Distinct from the Gemini-Live VoiceBot, which FEAT-213 explicitly deferred.

localization.role: Lazy TTS backend dispatch — extension point for a Supertonic branch
Abstract TTS contract a SupertonicTTSBackend must implement
Literal backend enum + mime_format to extend for 'supertonic'
Existing STT entrypoint to transcribe the inbound WS audio buffer
Reusable aiohttp WS shell (auth, sessions, _handle_audio_data); currently hard-wired to Gemini-Live VoiceBot
Source of the speakable .response (str) vs non-speakable .output/.data/.media
Wrapper returned by Agents — response: AIMessage, data, output
Precedent for the audio_base64 WS reply payload
Native Gemini-Live S2S bot — the path this feature is NOT (out of scope)

constraints.text: AIMessage.response is the only field safe to feed to TTS; output/data/media are Any (dataframes/markdown/JSON) and must travel as a separate structured 'content' block, not through the synthesizer.
constraints.implication: Split the WS reply: synthesize from .response, ship .output/.data/.media for display.

constraints.text: The voice stack (parrot.voice.*) lives in the ai-parrot-integrations distribution, not core.
constraints.implication: A generic AgentTalk voice endpoint creates a core->integrations dependency; either host the handler in integrations or guard the import (extras-gated) like the integrations themselves.

constraints.text: VoiceChatHandler.bot_factory is typed Callable[[], VoiceBot] and routes audio into a native Gemini session (audio_queue/gemini_responding).
constraints.implication: Supporting a text Agent + synthesizer requires generalizing the factory/dispatch (a 'pipeline' mode) or a sibling handler reusing its auth/session/recording code.

constraints.text: FEAT-213 reserved elevenlabs/openai backends as ValueError stubs and built the TTS layer mirroring the transcriber; faster-whisper (STT) is an optional/extras dependency.
constraints.implication: Supertonic (ONNX, model weights) should be an extras-gated optional backend, with graceful degradation to text-only or Google TTS if unavailable (FEAT-213 G5).

constraints.text: Audio crosses the WS as base64-in-JSON (both inbound _handle_audio_data and outbound to_websocket_message).
constraints.implication: Keep the same envelope: inbound {type:'audio_data', data:<b64>}, outbound {type:'voice_response', audio_base64, audio_format, content}.

recent_history.note: voice.py untouched since the monorepo move — VoiceBot is stable, not under active churn.

whats_new: An AgentTalk voice round-trip path: decode WS audio -> VoiceTranscriber -> run a text Agent -> VoiceSynthesizer.synthesize(AIMessage.response) -> reply {audio_base64 + content}.
Either a new 'pipeline/agent' mode on VoiceChatHandler or a sibling AgentTalkVoiceHandler reusing its auth/session/recording code.
whats_changed: TTSConfig.backend Literal extended to include 'supertonic'; VoiceSynthesizer._get_backend gains a 'supertonic' branch (F008).
VoiceChatHandler generalized so the inbound audio can be routed to a text-Agent+synthesizer instead of only a Gemini VoiceBot (F007).
whats_untouched: VoiceBot / GeminiLiveClient native speech-to-speech path (explicitly out of scope; FEAT-213 deferred it).
The STT layer internals (VoiceTranscriber/FasterWhisper) — reused as-is.
patterns_to_follow: Mirror FEAT-213: decoupled AbstractTTSBackend + lazy VoiceSynthesizer dispatch; graceful degradation to text-only on TTS failure (G5).
Mirror LiveVoiceResponse.to_websocket_message for the outbound audio_base64 envelope (F004).
integration_risks: Supertonic adds heavy optional deps + model weights; gate behind an extra and degrade gracefully if missing (F005, F008).
core->integrations dependency direction for a generic web voice endpoint (F002).
Browser audio playback format: TTSConfig.mime_format defaults to audio/ogg (Telegram-oriented); browsers may need audio/wav or audio/webm (F008).

confidence_map.claim: A decoupled STT+TTS stack (parrot.voice.transcriber + parrot.voice.tts) already exists and is reusable.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of package __init__ files + FEAT-213 spec.

confidence_map.claim: AIMessage.response (str) is the correct, isolated TTS source; output/data are non-speakable.
confidence_map.reasoning: Direct read of the field definitions + docstrings.

confidence_map.claim: A full aiohttp WS handler (VoiceChatHandler) with auth/sessions/base64 audio already exists.
VoiceChatHandler is hard-wired to the Gemini-Live VoiceBot and must be generalized (or mirrored) for a text-Agent pipeline.
confidence_map.reasoning: bot_factory typed Callable[[], VoiceBot]; audio routed into native gemini session.

confidence_map.claim: Adding Supertonic is one new AbstractTTSBackend + one Literal/dispatch entry.
confidence_map.reasoning: Read of synthesizer dispatch + abstract contract + TTSConfig.

confidence_map.claim: The audio-over-WS envelope is base64-in-JSON in both directions.
VoiceBot/Gemini Live native S2S is out of scope (deferred by FEAT-213).
Supertonic delivers the sub-second TTS latency the requester wants on-device.
confidence_map.reasoning: Claimed by the upstream project; not verified in-repo (no web fetch). Latency depends on hardware/ONNX runtime.

unknowns.question: Transport strategy: add a 'pipeline/agent' mode to the existing VoiceChatHandler, or ship a sibling AgentTalkVoiceHandler that reuses its auth/session/recording code?
plausible_answers: Extend VoiceChatHandler with a mode flag (less code, one endpoint)
Sibling handler (cleaner separation, no risk to the Gemini path)
Refactor shared parts into a base class both use
unknowns.user_answer: Sibling handler — ship a new AgentTalkVoiceHandler that reuses VoiceChatHandler's auth/session/recording code, keeping the Gemini-Live path untouched.

unknowns.question: Supertonic backend now, or ship the round-trip first on the existing Google TTS backend and add Supertonic as a follow-up?
plausible_answers: Supertonic from day one (the whole point is sub-second TTS)
Google TTS first (de-risk), Supertonic as a second task
Both behind config; default Google, opt-in Supertonic
unknowns.user_answer: Supertonic from day one — add SupertonicTTSBackend now, extras-gated, with graceful degradation to text-only on failure/unavailability.

unknowns.question: Single buffered round-trip (audio in -> one audio+content out) or streamed/partial audio chunks back?
plausible_answers: Stream partial TTS audio as the agent answers
unknowns.user_answer: Single buffered round-trip — audio in -> one {audio_base64 + content} reply.

unknowns.question: Which Agent answers the transcribed question — resolved per agent_id from the WS (like other handlers) via Agent.invoke() returning AgentResponse/AIMessage, or a single configured default agent?
plausible_answers: Per agent_id from the URL/auth, via BotManager
Caller passes agent_id in the start_session message
unknowns.user_answer: Per agent_id via BotManager — resolve the agent from agent_id (URL/auth) and call Agent.invoke(), consuming the returned AgentResponse/AIMessage.

unknowns.question: Output audio format for browser playback — keep TTSConfig.mime_format default audio/ogg, or default to audio/wav / audio/webm for AgentTalk's web player?
unknowns.user_answer: Default audio/wav for the web player (broadest browser support), negotiable via the start_session message. (Resolved by agent default — non-blocking.)

recommended_next_command.rationale: Localization is high-confidence and every building block exists; this is bounded enrichment. The remaining choices (handler strategy, Supertonic-now-vs-later, audio format) are implementation decisions a spec can pin down, not architectural unknowns requiring brainstorming.

alternatives.when: if you want to weigh the transport-strategy fork (extend VoiceChatHandler vs sibling handler) and streaming-vs-buffered as alternative architectures before committing.
